items.title: What is Suspended function in kotlin?
items.author: Shahid Iqbal
items.description:
In Kotlin, a suspend function is a special type of function that can be paused and resumed later, allowing for asynchronous and non-blocking execution. Suspend functions are used in coroutines to manage concurrency and avoid blocking the main thread, which is important in Android for ensuring smooth and responsive user experiences.

A suspend function is defined using the suspend keyword and can only be called from within another suspend function or from a coroutine. When a suspend function is called, it can pause its execution and return control to the caller, while preserving its state. The function can later be resumed and continue its execution from where it left off.

suspend fun delaying() {
        //Code
}

Here’s an example of how a suspend function can be used in Android to fetch data from a network:

suspend fun fetchData(): List<Data> {
    // Code to fetch data from network
    // ...
    return dataList
}

In this example, the fetchData function is defined as a suspend function and can be used to fetch data from a network. The function can be called from a coroutine, which allows it to run asynchronously and avoid blocking the main thread. The data can then be used to update the UI or perform other actions as needed.

Note that the suspend function must be called from a coroutine or another suspend function. In Android, this can be achieved by using the launch or async functions, for example:

GlobalScope().launch {
    val data = fetchData()
    // Use data to update UI, etc.
    withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
        // Update UI here
    }
}

Well That’s All For Now Bye Bye!!
